skill.name: jira-automation
skill.description: Automate Jira tasks through Rube MCP and Composio, including issues, projects, sprints, boards, comments, users, and roles.
skill.summary: Guide Jira automation workflows through Rube MCP with current tool schemas.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography findings, but review shows they are markdown code spans and ordinary Jira terms, not executable code. The real risk is the required connection to an external Rube MCP endpoint and authorized Jira operations that can create, edit, assign, comment on, and move issues. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, hidden script, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Network Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is a legitimate setup step for the stated Jira automation purpose, but it creates a dependency on an external service that can mediate Jira OAuth and tool calls. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The URL is explicitly present and semantically required by the workflow; the risk is dependency and authorization exposure, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external MCP endpoint is explicitly documented as required. The context supports a legitimate integration, but users must trust the third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Jira Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill guides issue creation, editing, assignment, sprint movement, comment creation, and project role updates. These are expected Jira automation functions, but mistakes or overbroad permissions can modify production project data. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The listed tool sequences directly include write actions; the risk depends on the user's Jira permissions and approval flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines list Jira tools that change issues, sprints, comments, and roles. This is not malicious, but it is a material operational risk.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Matches
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by markdown code spans around MCP and Jira tool names, parameters, JQL examples, and quick reference entries. No shell execution, Ruby backticks, script file, or command interpreter invocation appears in SKILL.md. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: Manual review found documentation-only backticks and no executable source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks wrap markdown identifiers only. There is no code path that executes external commands.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines contain metadata or Jira text such as description, issue description, Atlassian Document Format, and ORDER BY created DESC, not cryptographic algorithms. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The scanner appears to match substrings like desc; manual context shows no hashing or encryption usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is plain Jira documentation. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires configuring an external MCP endpoint at rube.app. Users should verify the endpoint and understand what Jira data and actions are exposed through the OAuth connection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is directly cited in setup instructions. The security concern is trust in the external integration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive or Persistent Jira Changes
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflows include actions that persist changes in Jira, including creating issues, editing fields, assigning issues, moving issues to sprints, adding comments, and adding users to roles.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow names are explicit write operations. Their impact is bounded by Jira authorization and user approval.

content.user_title: Automate Jira Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Jira work often requires repeated searches, updates, comments, and sprint changes across many projects. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows using current Jira tool schemas.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for a Rube MCP connection to Jira.
Defines tool sequences for searching Jira issues with JQL.
Explains how to create, edit, and assign Jira issues.
Covers sprint and board workflows for Jira Software.
Documents comment, project, user, and project role operations.
Provides practical notes about fields, pagination, account IDs, and ADF.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Jira OAuth connection.
Does not include executable scripts or automated validation code.
Depends on current Jira tool schemas from RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS.
Can only perform actions allowed by the connected Jira account.

use_cases.title: Triage Issue Backlogs
use_cases.description: Search Jira issues with JQL, inspect details, and prepare follow-up actions for large project backlogs.

use_cases.title: Update Sprint Work
use_cases.description: Find boards and sprints, move issues into the right sprint, and create new sprints when needed.

use_cases.title: Automate Routine Issue Changes
use_cases.description: Create tasks, update fields, assign owners, and add comments through approved Jira tool calls.

prompt_templates.title: Find Open Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Jira automation skill to search for open issues in project PROJ. Show issue keys, summaries, status, and assignees.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Jira automation skill to create a Task in project PROJ with this summary and description. Confirm required fields before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Sprint Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Jira automation skill to list boards, find the active sprint, and move selected issue keys into that sprint after I approve the list.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Batch Update Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Jira automation skill to search issues matching a JQL filter, summarize proposed edits, and only apply updates after explicit approval.

output_examples.input: Find bugs created in the last week for project PROJ.
output: A concise issue list with keys, summaries, priorities, statuses, and assignees.
A note when pagination indicates more matching issues are available.
Suggested next steps, such as opening details for selected issue keys.

output_examples.input: Create a story and assign it to a developer.
output: A short confirmation of the project, issue type, summary, and required fields.
A request for approval before creating or assigning the issue.
The created issue key and assignment result after approved tool calls.

output_examples.input: Move these issues into the next sprint.
output: The matching board and sprint options found through Jira.
A preflight list of issue keys that will be moved.
A completion summary showing which issues moved successfully.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before each workflow to confirm current Jira schemas.
Review project fields, issue types, and account IDs before write actions.
Ask for explicit approval before creating, editing, assigning, or moving Jira data.
anti_patterns: Do not assume field names, custom field IDs, or issue types are consistent across projects.
Do not run broad JQL searches without pagination limits.
Do not apply batch Jira updates before showing the exact proposed changes.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Jira?
faq.answer: It guides Jira actions through Rube MCP and Composio. Users still need an active Jira connection.

faq.question: Can it create or edit Jira issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for creating issues, editing fields, assigning owners, and adding comments.

faq.question: Can it manage sprints?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers listing boards, listing sprints, moving issues to sprints, and creating sprints.

faq.question: Does it include runnable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown workflow guide for MCP tool usage.

faq.question: Why must tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps use the current parameters.

faq.question: What permissions are required?
faq.answer: The connected Jira account must have permission for each requested read or write operation.
